title: America Supports Taiwan Act
plain_summary: This new law says that all US government offices must call Taiwan by its real name "Taiwan" instead of using "Chinese Taipei" when they talk about it or write about it on websites. The government thinks calling it "Chinese Taipei" might make people think that China owns Taiwan, which could cause confusion. By using the name "Taiwan" instead, the US wants to help support peaceful talks between Taiwan and mainland China. This change will happen in all government communications and official documents.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the America Supports Taiwan Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings and Purpose
sections.text: Congress has found several important facts. The United States Government has never officially agreed that the People's Republic of China owns Taiwan. The People's Republic of China is led by the Chinese Communist Party and is trying to control Taiwan through convincing and forcing them. The People's Liberation Army wants to be able to invade Taiwan by two thousand twenty seven, which marks one hundred years since the Chinese Communist Party's military was founded. The People's Republic of China calls Taiwan a region and calls Taiwan's President the leader of the Taiwan region, showing they believe Taiwan belongs to them. Taiwan and mainland China are separated by an invisible middle line in the Taiwan Strait that both sides generally respected from nineteen ninety nine until September two thousand twenty. Since then, Chinese military aircraft and ships have crossed this line many times, with more than one thousand four hundred Chinese aircraft crossing the line in two thousand twenty four alone. Chinese military aircraft flying into Taiwan's Air Defense Identification Zone have increased sharply over time, from about three hundred ninety times in two thousand twenty to about three thousand seventy five times in two thousand twenty four. Many people in Taiwan see the name Chinese Taipei as a symbol of oppression from the People's Republic of China. This name originally came from trying to find a way for both Taiwan and the People's Republic of China to participate in the nineteen eighty Olympics. In the Chinese language, Taiwan uses a version of Chinese Taipei where Chinese refers to culture, not government control. However, the English translation can be misunderstood to suggest that the People's Republic of China owns Taipei and all of Taiwan. Congress believes the United States must keep the promises it made in the Taiwan Relations Act, which says the United States will maintain the ability to resist any force that would threaten the security or economic system of the people on Taiwan. Congress also believes the United States Government should continue supporting Taiwan so it can defend itself against increasing pressure from the People's Republic of China. Finally, Congress believes the United States Government should stop using the name Chinese Taipei and instead use Taiwan to avoid suggesting ownership and to support peaceful solutions to differences between Taiwan and mainland China.

sections.title: Section 3 Agency Requirement to Use Taiwan
sections.text: The head of any government agency cannot use the term Chinese Taipei and must use Taiwan instead. There are only two exceptions to this rule. First, agencies can use Chinese Taipei when explaining the history of how the People's Republic of China has tried to control Taiwan through convincing and forcing them. Second, agencies can use Chinese Taipei when working with international organizations where Taiwan participates under a different official name. Within fourteen days after this law is passed, the head of each agency must make sure their agency's website follows these rules. In this section, agency means any government department or office as defined in section five hundred fifty one of title five of the United States Code.
